evals.prompt: I'm building a new skill for ADHD executive function support — it should help with task prioritization, time blindness compensation, and working memory scaffolding. I need it to work across Claude, OpenAI, n8n, and as an MCP server. Can you scaffold the full project with the build system?
evals.expected_output: A complete project scaffold: source/manifest.json with metadata wrapper and JSON Schema parameters, build.ts generating all 6 formats, package.json with per-format scripts, and source markdown stubs.

evals.prompt: I have an existing dyscalculia-support-skill project. Its manifest uses the older flat format with CommandParameter arrays. I want to migrate it to the standardized manifest schema and regenerate the build.ts.
evals.expected_output: Migration of flat manifest to metadata-wrapper + JSON Schema parameters. New domain-agnostic build.ts. All 6 formats build.

evals.prompt: I've got a tarot card reading interpretation skill. It has commands for single-card pulls, three-card spreads, Celtic Cross readings, and card-of-the-day. Generate the multi-format build system for it.
evals.expected_output: Complete build system for a non-educational domain. build.ts must be domain-agnostic. Manifest with metadata, skills, and commands with JSON Schema parameters.

evals.prompt: I have a dyslexia support skill that I need to make available in English, Spanish, French, German, and Arabic. It has commands for text-simplification, reading-guide-generation, and font-recommendation. Scaffold the project with full i18n support including RTL for Arabic.
evals.expected_output: Project with i18n config in manifest (defaultLocale: en, locales: [en, es, fr, de, ar]). Locale JSON stubs for es, fr, de, ar in source/i18n/. build.ts generates locale subdirectories for all 6 formats. Arabic output includes RTL direction metadata. No domain strings hardcoded in build.ts.

evals.prompt: I want to add internationalization to my existing dyscalculia-support-skill project. It currently has no i18n. I need Spanish and Portuguese translations, and I also want to provide localized markdown content for both languages. Can you add i18n support?
evals.expected_output: Added i18n field to manifest with localizedMarkdown: true. Created source/i18n/es.json and source/i18n/pt.json with translated strings. Created source/i18n/es/skills/, source/i18n/es/commands/, source/i18n/pt/skills/, source/i18n/pt/commands/ directories with localized markdown stubs. build.ts loads locale files and localized markdown, generating locale-specific outputs.

evals.prompt: Build only the French MCP server for my skill project. The manifest already has i18n configured with fr in the locales array.
evals.expected_output: Ran tsx build.ts --format mcp --locale fr. Generated dist/mcp-server/fr/ with French-localized tool descriptions, knowledge files, and package.json. No other formats or locales built.

iteration_1.eval_2_dyscalculia: 9/10 — domain term leaked into CLI section of build.ts
iteration_1.eval_3_tarot: 9/10 — domain term leaked into CLI section of build.ts
iteration_1.fix_applied: Strengthened SKILL.md and build-template.md with explicit domain-agnostic guidance for CLI generator
